\"sort_by\": \"correctness\"\n}\n\n\ndef load_config(config_path: str = None) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"Load configuration from file or use defaults.\"\"\"\n    config = DEFAULT_CONFIG.copy()\n\n    if config_path and Path(config_path).exists():\n        with open(config_path, 'r') as f:\n            user_config = json.load(f)\n            config.update(user_config)\n\n    # Expand tilde in paths\n    if 'declarative_config_path' in config:\n        config['declarative_config_path'] = os.path.expanduser(config['declarative_config_path'])\n\n    return config\n\n\ndef run_script(script_path: str, args: List[str] = None) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Run a Python script and return its stdout.\"\"\"\n    script_full_path = Path(__file__).parent / script_path\n    if not script_full_path.exists():\n        raise FileNotFoundError(f\"Script not found: {script_full_path}\")\n\n    cmd = [sys.executable, str(script_full_path)]\n    if args:\n        cmd.extend(args)\n\n    result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=60)\n\n    if result.returncode != 0:\n        raise RuntimeError(f\"Script {script_path} failed: {result.stderr}\")\n\n    return result.stdout\n\n\ndef fetch_releases(config: Dict[str, Any]) -> List[Dict]:\n    \"\"\"Fetch releases from GitHub.\"\"\"\n    output = run_script(\"github-release-fetcher.py\")\n    releases = json.loads(output)\n\n    return releases\n\n\ndef find_deployments(config: Dict[str, Any]) -> List[Dict]:\n    \"\"\"Find ARMOR deployments in declarative-config.\"\"\"\n    print(\"Finding ARMOR deployments...\", file=sys.stderr)\n\n    declarative_path = config['declarative_config_path']\n    if not os.path.exists(declarative_path):\n        raise FileNotFoundError(f\"declarative-config not found at {declarative_path}\")\n\n    output = run_script(\"find-armor-deployments.py\", [declarative_path])\n    deployments = json.loads(output)\n\n    print(f\"Found {len(deployments)} deployment(s)\", file=sys.stderr)\n\n    return deployments\n\n\ndef get_current_version_from_repo() -> Dict:\n    \"\"\"Get current ARMOR version from local VERSION file and git.\"\"\"\n    try:\n        version_file = Path(__file__).parent.parent / 'VERSION'\n        if not version_file.exists():\n            print(\"Warning: VERSION file not found, using GitHub releases only\", file=sys.stderr)\n            return None\n\n        version_str = version_file.read_text().strip()\n        print(f\"Current version from repo: {version_str}\", file=sys.stderr)\n\n        # Try to get commit date for this version\n        import re\n        match = re.match(r'0\\.1\\.(\\d+)', version_str)\n        if match:\n            version_num = int(match.group(1))\n\n            # Try to find the commit for this version\n            result = subprocess.run(\n                [\"git\", \"log\", \"--all\", \"--grep\", f\"auto-bump version to 0.1.{version_num}\",\n                 \"--format=%ci\", \"-n\", \"1\"],\n                cwd=Path(__file__).parent.parent,\n                capture_output=True,\n                text=True,\n                timeout=10\n            )\n\n            commit_date = None\n            if result.returncode == 0 and result.stdout.strip():\n                date_str = result.stdout.strip()\n                try:\n                    commit_date = datetime.fromisoformat(date_str.replace(' +', '+')).isoformat()\n                except ValueError:\n                    pass\n\n            return {\n                'tag': version_str,\n                'published_at': commit_date or datetime.now().isoformat(),\n                'is_correctness': False,  # Will be determined later\n                'url': ''\n            }\n    except Exception as e:\n        print(f\"Warning: Could not read VERSION file: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n\n    return None\n\n\ndef compare_drift(\n    deployments: List[Dict],\n    releases: List[Dict],\n    config: Dict[str, Any]\n) -> List[Dict]:\n    \"\"\"Compare deployments against releases and generate drift reports.\"\"\"\n    print(\"Comparing drift...\", file=sys.stderr)\n\n    # Write temporary files for compare script\n    import tempfile\n\n    with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(mode='w', suffix='.json', delete=False) as f:\n        json.dump(deployments, f)\n        deployments_file = f.name\n\n    with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(mode='w', suffix='.json', delete=False) as f:\n        json.dump(releases, f)\n        releases_file = f.name\n\n    try:\n        args = [\n            '--deployments', deployments_file,\n            '--releases', releases_file,\n            '--releases-threshold', str(config['releases_threshold']),\n            '--days-threshold', str(config['days_threshold']),\n            '--json'\n        ]\n\n        output = run_script(\"compare-version-drift.py\", args)\n        result = json.loads(output)\n\n        # Sort by specified field\n        sort_by = config.get('sort_by', 'correctness')\n        deployments_list = result.get('deployments', [])\n\n        if sort_by == 'correctness':\n            deployments_list.sort(key=lambda d: d.get('is_correctness_drift', False), reverse=True)\n        elif sort_by == 'releases':\n            deployments_list.sort(key=lambda d: d.get('releases_behind') or 0, reverse=True)\n        elif sort_by == 'days':\n            deployments_list.sort(key=lambda d: d.get('days_behind') or 0, reverse=True)\n        elif sort_by == 'cluster':\n            deployments_list.sort(key=lambda d: d.get('cluster', ''))\n\n        result['deployments'] = deployments_list\n        return result\n\n    finally:\n        # Clean up temp files\n        os.unlink(deployments_file)\n        os.unlink(releases_file)\n\n\ndef format_report(drift_result: Dict, config: Dict) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Format human-readable report.\"\"\"\n    lines = []\n    lines.append(\"=\" * 80)\n    lines.append(\"ARMOR Version Drift Report\")\n    lines.append(\"=\" * 80)\n    lines.append(f\"Generated: {datetime.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S UTC')}\")\n    lines.append(f\"Thresholds: > {config['releases_threshold']} releases, > {config['days_threshold']} days\")\n    lines.append(\"\")\n\n    summary = drift_result.get('summary', {})\n    lines.append(f\"Total deployments: {summary.get('total_deployments', 0)}\")\n    lines.append(f\"With drift: {summary.get('with_drift', 0)}\")\n    lines.append(f\"With correctness drift: {summary.get('with_correctness_drift', 0)}\")\n    lines.append(\"\")\n    lines.append(\"=\" * 80)\n    lines.append(\"\")\n\n    for deployment in drift_result.get('deployments', []):\n        if deployment.get('is_correctness_drift'):\n            icon = \"🔴\"\n        elif deployment.get('is_drift'):\n            icon = \"🟡\"\n        else:\n            icon = \"✅\"\n\n        lines.append(f\"{icon} {deployment.get('cluster', 'unknown')}\")\n        lines.append(f\"   Deployed: {deployment.get('deployed_tag', 'N/A')}\")\n        lines.append(f\"   Latest:   {deployment.get('latest_tag', 'N/A')}\")\n\n        if deployment.get('releases_behind') is not None:\n            lines.append(f\"   Releases behind: {deployment['releases_behind']}\")\n\n        if deployment.get('days_behind') is not None:\n            lines.append(f\"   Days behind: {deployment['days_behind']}\")\n\n        if deployment.get('is_correctness_drift'):\n            lines.append(f\"   🚨 CORRECTNESS DRIFT: Missing correctness releases!\")\n\n        lines.append(\"\")\n\n    return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n\n\ndef main():\n    \"\"\"Main entry point.\"\"\"\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(\n        description='ARMOR Version Drift Check - Unified Script'\n    )\n    parser.add_argument(\n        '--config',\n        help='Path to configuration file (JSON)'\n    )\n    parser.add_argument(\n        '--json',\n        action='store_true',\n        help='Output machine-readable JSON instead of human-readable format'\n    )\n    parser.add_argument(\n        '--output',\n        help='Write report to file (in addition to stdout)'\n    )\n    parser.add_argument(\n        '--releases-threshold',\n        type=int,\n        help='Override releases threshold from config'\n    )\n    parser.add_argument(\n        '--days-threshold',\n        type=int,\n        help='Override days threshold from config'\n    )\n    parser.add_argument(\n        '--sort-by',\n        choices=['cluster', 'releases', 'days', 'correctness'],\n        help='Sort output by field'\n    )\n\n    args = parser.parse_args()\n\n    try:\n        # Load configuration\n        config = load_config(args.config)\n\n        # Override config with command-line arguments\n        if args.releases_threshold:\n            config['releases_threshold'] = args.releases_threshold\n        if args.days_threshold:\n            config['days_threshold'] = args.days_threshold\n        if args.sort_by:\n            config['sort_by'] = args.sort_by\n\n        # Get current version from local VERSION file (more accurate for ARMOR)\n        current_version = get_current_version_from_repo()\n\n        # Fetch GitHub releases/tags\n        releases = fetch_releases(config)\n\n        # If we have a current version from VERSION file, prepend it to releases\n        if current_version:\n            # Check if VERSION file version is newer than fetched releases\n            releases.insert(0, current_version)\n\n        deployments = find_deployments(config)\n        drift_result = compare_drift(deployments, releases, config)\n\n        # Add metadata\n        drift_result['generated_at'] = datetime.now().isoformat()\n        drift_result['config'] = {\n            'releases_threshold': config['releases_threshold'],\n            'days_threshold': config['days_threshold']\n        }\n\n        # Output\n        if args.json:\n            output = json.dumps(drift_result, indent=2)\n        else:\n            output = format_report(drift_result, config)\n\n        print(output)\n\n        # Write to file if specified\n        if args.output:\n            output_path = Path(args.output)\n            output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n            output_path.write_text(output)\n            print(f\"Report written to {args.output}\", file=sys.stderr)\n\n        # Exit with error code if there's any drift (exceeds thresholds)\n        if drift_result.get('summary', {}).get('with_drift', 0) > 0:\n            sys.exit(1)\n\n        sys.exit(0)\n\n    except Exception as e:\n ARMOR Scripts\n\nThis directory contains utility scripts for ARMOR operations and monitoring.\n\n## Version Drift Check\n\nAutomated version drift monitoring across ARMOR deployments.\n\n### Files\n\n- `check-version-drift.sh` - **Main orchestrator script** (recommended)\n- `version-drift-check.py` - Python pipeline that wires together all components\n- `github-release-fetcher.py` - Fetches releases from GitHub API\n- `find-armor-deployments.py` - Discovers ARMOR deployments in declarative-config\n- `compare-version-drift.py` - Compares deployments against releases\n- `armor-version-drift-check.cron` - Cron job definition\n- `setup-version-drift-schedule.sh` - Setup script to install cron job\n\n### Usage\n\nRun the check field:\n```bash\n./scripts/check-version-drift.sh --sort-by cluster\n./scripts/check-version-drift.sh --sort-by correctness\n```\n\n### Options\n\n- `--releases N` - Flag deployments N or more releases behind (default: 3)\n- `--days N` - Flag deployments N or more days behind (default: 30)\n- `--json` - Output machine-readable JSON instead of human-readable format\n- `--output FILE` - Write report to file (in addition to stdout)\n- `--sort-by FIELD` - Sort output by field: cluster, releases, days, correctness (default: correctness)\n- `--config FILE` - Use configuration file (JSON)\n- `--help` - Show help message\n\n### Exit Codes\n\n- `0` - All deployments within thresholds\n- `1` - One or more deployments exceed thresholds\n- `2` - Error occurred\n\n### What It Checks\n\nThe script scans ARMOR deployments across all clusters in `jedarden/declarative-config` and:\n1. **Discovery**: Finds all ARMOR deployment files using `find-armor-deployments.py`\n2. **Fetching**: Fetches latest GitHub releases using `github-release-fetcher.py`\n3. **Comparison**: Compares deployed versions against releases using `compare-version-drift.py`\n4. **Flagging**: Flags deployments needing attention based on thresholds\n\n### What It Checks\n\nThe script scans ARMOR deployments across all clusters in `jedarden/declarative-config` and:\n1. Reads the deployed ARMOR image tag from each deployment file\n2. Compares against the current ARMOR version (from `VERSION` file)\n3. Calculates version drift (versions behind, days behind)\n4. Checks for correctness/security releases between versions\n5. Flags deployments needing attention\n\n### Clusters Checked\n\n- iad-ci\n- iad-kalshi\n- rs-manager\n- ord-devimprint\n- iad-native-ads\n- iad-acb\n\n### Thresholds\n\nBy default, deployments are flagged if:\n- More than 50 versions behind current\n- More than 30 days behind current\n- Any correctness/security releases missed\n\nThese thresholds can be adjusted in the script.\n\n### Scheduling\n\nTo schedule daily checks (if cron is available):\n```bash\nbash scripts/setup-version-drift-schedule.sh\n```\n\nThe check runs daily at 9:17 AM (avoiding :00/:00 load spikes).\n\nLogs are written to `logs/version-drift-check.log`.\n\n### Output\n\nThe script outputs:\n- Human-readable summary to stdout\n- Detailed JSON when run with `--json` flag\n- Flags for:\n  - 🔴 Deployments needing update\n  - 🔶 Non-version tags (git SHAs)\n  - ⚠️  Missed correctness releases\n\n## GitHub Release Fetcher\n\nFetches ARMOR releases from GitHub API and distinguishes correctness-labeled releases from routine version bumps.\n\n### Files\n\n- `github-release-fetcher.py` - Fetches and categorizes releases\n\n### Usage\n\nRun the fetcher:\n```bash\npython3 scripts/github-release-fetcher.py\n```\n\n### Output\n\nReturns structured JSON with release information:\n```json\n[\n  {\n    \"tag\": \"v0.1.43\",\n    \"published_at\": \"2026-03-28T13:09:18Z\",\n    \"is_correctness\": true,\n    \"url\": \"https://github.com/jedarden/ARMOR/releases/tag/v0.1.43\"\n  }\n]\n```\n\nFields:\n- `tag` - Release tag name\n- `published_at` - ISO 8601 timestamp when published\n- `is_correctness` - Boolean indicating if release is correctness/security-labeled\n- `url` - GitHub release page URL\n\n### Correctness Detection\n\nReleases are classified as correctness-labeled if any of these keywords appear in the tag name, release title, or release notes:\n- correctness\n- fix\n- critical\n- security\n- bug\n- patch\n- hotfix\n- urgent\n- vulnerability\n- cve\n- issue\n- regression\n\n### Error Handling\n\nThe script handles:\n- GitHub API rate limits (includes small delays between paginated requests)\n- Network errors and timeouts\n- JSON parsing errors\n- Empty release lists\n\nAll errors are reported to stderr with non-zero exit codes.\n\n## Version Drift Comparison\n\nCompares deployed ARMOR versions against GitHub releases to detect drift.\n\n### Files\n\n- `compare-version-drift.py` - Main comparison script that integrates outputs from github-release-fetcher.py and find-armor-deployments.py\n\n### Usage\n\nBasic usage with default thresholds:\n```bash\npython3 scripts/compare-version-drift.py \\\n  --deployments <path-to-deployments.json> \\\n  --releases <path-to-releases.json>\n```\n\nWith custom thresholds:\n```bash\npython3 scripts/compare-version-drift.py \\\n  --deployments deployments.json \\\n  --releases releases.json \\\n  --releases-threshold 10 \\\n  --days-threshold 7\n```\n\nOutput machine-readable JSON:\n```bash\npython3 scripts/compare-version-drift.py \\\n  --deployments deployments.json \\\n  --releases releases.json \\\n  --json\n```\n\nSort by different fields:\n```bash\n# Sort by releases behind (most behind first)\npython3 scripts/compare-version-drift.py \\\n  --deployments deployments.json \\\n  --releases releases.json \\\n  --sort-by releases\n\n# Sort by correctness drift priority\npython3 scripts/compare-version-drift.py \\\n  --deployments deployments.json \\\n  --releases releases.json \\\n  --sort-by correctness\n```\n\n### Integration\n\nThe comparison script integrates the outputs from both child scripts:\n\n```bash\n# Generate both inputs\npython3 scripts/github-release-fetcher.py > /tmp/releases.json\npython3 scripts/find-armor-deployments.py > /tmp/deployments.json\n\n# Run comparison\npython3 scripts/compare-version-drift.py \\\n  --deployments /tmp/deployments.json \\\n  --releases /tmp/releases.json\n```\n\n### Output Format\n\nThe script produces a structured report for each cluster:\n\n**Human-readable output:**\n- Shows cluster status with emoji indicators (🔴 drift, 🟡 warning, ✅ OK)\n- Displays deployed vs latest versions\n- Shows releases behind and days behind counts\n- Highlights correctness drift with 🚨 emoji\n- Summary statistics\n\n**JSON output:**\n```json\n{\n  \"thresholds\": {\n    \"releases\": 50,\n    \"days\": 30\n  },\n  \"summary\": {\n    \"total_deployments\": 6,\n    \"with_drift\": 3,\n    \"with_correctness_drift\": 2\n  },\n  \"deployments\": [\n    {\n      \"cluster\": \"iad-ci\",\n      \"deployed_tag\": \"0.1.24\",\n      \"latest_tag\": \"v0.1.50\",\n      \"releases_behind\": 2,\n      \"days_behind\": null,\n      \"is_drift\": true,\n      \"is_correctness_drift\": true,\n      \"deployed_date\": null,\n      \"latest_date\": \"2026-07-15T16:00:00Z\",\n      \"filepath\": \"/path/to/deployment.yml\"\n    }\n  ]\n}\n```\n\n### Acceptance Criteria\n\n✅ **Configurable thresholds**: Customizable N releases and M days thresholds via CLI arguments\n✅ **Structured reports**: Per-cluster deployment comparison with all required metrics\n✅ **Correctness priority**: Correctness drift flagged with highest priority (🚨 emoji)\n✅ **Machine-readable JSON**: Full JSON output with deployment details and summary statistics\n\n### Exit Codes\n\n- `0` - Success (even if drift is detected)\n- `1` - Error (invalid inputs, file parsing errors, etc.)\n","numLines":295,"startLine":1,"totalLines":295}}}

bf-2t1f: ARMOR Version Drift Check Implementation\n\n## Overview\n\nImplemented comprehensive version drift monitoring across all ARMOR deployments in jedarden/declarative-config.\n\n## What Was Implemented\n\n### Core Components\n\n1. **Unified Drift Check Script** (`scripts/version-drift-check.py`)\n   - Orchestrates the complete pipeline\n   - Fetches GitHub releases\n   - Scans declarative-config for ARMOR deployments\n   - Compares and reports drift\n\n2. **GitHub Release Fetcher** (`scripts/github-release-fetcher.py`)\n   - Fetches releases from GitHub API\n   - Detects correctness-labeled releases via keyword matching\n   - Returns structured JSON with metadata\n\n3. **Deployment Discovery** (`scripts/find-armor-deployments.py`)\n   - Scans declarative-config for ARMOR deployment files\n   - Extracts image tags from each deployment\n   - Handles multiple file formats (.yml, .yaml)\n\n4. **Drift Comparison** (`scripts/compare-version-drift.py`)\n   - Compares deployed tags against latest releases\n   - Calculates releases behind and days behind\n   - Flags correctness drift separately (highest priority)\n   - Supports configurable thresholds\n\n5. **Scheduling Infrastructure**\n   - `scripts/setup-version-drift-schedule.sh` - Installs cron job\n   - `scripts/armor-version-drift-check.cron` - Cron template\n   - Runs daily at 9:17 AM (avoiding :00/:30 load spikes)\n\n### Clusters Monitored\n\n- iad-acb (AI Code Battle)\n- iad-ci (CI/CD cluster)\n- iad-kalshi (Kalshi workloads)\n- iad-native-ads (Native Ads)\n- ord-devimprint (DevImprint)\n- rs-manager (Rackspace Spot manager)\n\nNote: apexalgo-iad cluster exists but does not have an ARMOR deployment.\n\n### Features\n\n✅ **Configurable Thresholds**\n- `--releases N`: Flag deployments N+ releases behind (default: 3)\n- `--days N`: Flag deployments N+ days behind (default: 30)\n\n✅ **Correctness Detection**\n- Distinguishes correctness/security releases from routine bumps\n- Keywords: correctness, fix, critical, security, bug, patch, hotfix, urgent, vulnerability, cve, issue, regression\n- Correctness drift flagged with 🚨 emoji and highest priority\n\n✅ **Output Formats**\n- Human-readable table with emoji indicators (🔴 drift, 🟡 warning, ✅ OK)\n- JSON format for integration with monitoring/alerting\n- Sortable by cluster, releases, days, or correctness priority\n\n✅ **Scheduling**\n- Cron job setup script for automated daily checks\n- Logs written to `logs/version-drift-check.log`\n- Easy manual execution for ad-hoc checks\n\n### Usage Examples\n\n```bash\n# Run manually with defaults\npython3 scripts/version-drift-check.py\n\n# Custom thresholds\npython3 scripts/version-drift-check.py --releases 5 --days 60\n\n# JSON output for Slack/webhook integration\npython3 scripts/version-drift-check.py --json --output report.json\n\n# Sort by correctness priority\npython3 scripts/version-drift-check.py --sort-by correctness\n```\n\n### Exit Codes\n\n- `0`: All deployments within thresholds\n- `1`: One or more deployments exceed thresholds\n- `2`: Error occurred\n\n## Verification\n\nRan drift check on 2026-07-16:\n- Total deployments: 6\n- With drift: 0 (all within 50 releases / 30 days thresholds)\n- All clusters properly scanned\n- Correctness detection working\n\n## Documentation\n\nComprehensive README in `scripts/README.md` criteria met\n- Version drift tracking across all deployments\n- Correctness-labeled releases distinctly flagged\n- Configurable thresholds for releases and days\n- Scheduled check infrastructure in
